data.title: U.S. API Reports Significant Drop in Refined Oil Stocks
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a substantial decrease in U.S. refined oil inventories for the week ending April 17. According to Jin10, the stocks fell by 4.59 million barrels, surpassing the expected decline of 2.5 million barrels. The previous week's data showed a reduction of 3.363 million barrels. This larger-than-anticipated drop in inventories may influence market dynamics and oil prices.

data.title: U.S. Extends Ceasefire with Iran Amid Proposal Awaiting
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States will extend its ceasefire with Iran as it awaits a unified proposal from Iranian leaders. According to NS3.AI, Trump emphasized that the U.S. military will maintain the blockade and remain fully prepared during this pause in hostilities.

data.title: Meta Platforms Launches $1 Billion Data Center Project in Tulsa
data.description: Meta Platforms has initiated a $1 billion data center construction project in Tulsa, Oklahoma. According to Jin10, the Tulsa data center project includes over $25 million in local infrastructure improvements. It is expected to support more than 1,000 construction jobs at its peak and create approximately 100 operational positions upon completion.

data.title: Trump Endorses Businessman for New York House Seat
data.description: A businessman who gained attention last year with a notable “Vote for Trump” sign has received an endorsement from U.S. President Donald Trump. Bloomberg posted on X that the endorsement is for the race to fill Elise Stefanik’s House seat in upstate New York.

data.title: Ripple Plans Quantum-Ready Transition for XRP Ledger by 2028
data.description: Ripple has outlined a roadmap to prepare the XRP Ledger for quantum computing challenges by 2028. According to NS3.AI, the company has already initiated testing to ensure quantum resistance. The plan involves integrating quantum-resistant methods into the test network alongside existing security measures by late 2026, with a complete transition expected by 2028. Ripple has also announced an emergency fallback mechanism to disable traditional signatures should quantum risks emerge earlier than anticipated.

data.title: AI-Driven Scams Surge 500%, Experts Urge Countermeasures
data.description: AI-enabled scam activities have surged by 500% over the past year, according to witnesses at a recent congressional hearing. Experts highlighted that this increase is complicating the detection of crypto fraud. According to NS3.AI, authorities are being urged to leverage AI technology to combat these sophisticated attackers. One witness proposed that existing laws be reviewed to potentially apply terrorism designation powers to ransomware actors who target hospitals.

data.title: Polymarket Seeks Funding at $15 Billion Valuation Amid Competitive Landscape
data.description: Polymarket is reportedly in discussions to secure funding at a valuation of $15 billion. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as its competitor, Kalshi, was recently valued at $22 billion. Industry experts suggest that Polymarket's anticipated token launch and potential airdrop farming could complicate the interpretation of its trading volume as purely product demand.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 75,000 USDT with a 1.76% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 21, 2026, 19:43 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 75,000 USDT and is now trading at 74,910.6875 USDT, with a narrowed 1.76% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: SoFi Crypto Expands XRP Access Amid User Criticism
data.description: SoFi Crypto has introduced support for XRP deposits, broadening access to a token with an estimated market capitalization of $100 billion. According to NS3.AI, this move has been met with criticism from users who are dissatisfied with the platform's restriction on transferring XRP to external wallets. SoFi's support page confirms that transfers to outside wallets are not allowed.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Images2.0 Model
data.description: OpenAI has introduced its latest image model, ChatGPT Images2.0. According to Jin10, this new model aims to enhance the capabilities of ChatGPT by integrating advanced image processing features. The update is expected to improve the interaction between users and AI, allowing for more sophisticated image generation and analysis. OpenAI continues to push the boundaries of artificial intelligence, focusing on creating tools that can seamlessly blend text and visual content. This development marks another step forward in the evolution of AI-driven communication technologies.

data.title: Expert Warns Kelp DAO Hack May Impact Blockchain Adoption by Financial Firms
data.description: Jefferies analyst Andrew Moss has expressed concerns that the recent hack of Kelp DAO could hinder the adoption of blockchain technology by traditional financial institutions. According to NS3.AI, Moss highlighted that the incident revealed significant vulnerabilities in bridge systems and single points of failure. These issues may prompt financial firms to reevaluate their risk management strategies when considering blockchain integration.

data.title: Venezuelan Bond Investors Engage with Trump Administration on Economic Recovery
data.description: A group of Venezuelan bond investors recently met with U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration officials to discuss their potential involvement in Venezuela's economic recovery. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these talks as Venezuela faces ongoing economic challenges. The meeting marks a pivotal step in exploring how investors can contribute to stabilizing the nation's economy. The discussions focused on strategies to leverage bond investments for economic growth and recovery. This engagement underscores the international interest in Venezuela's economic future and the role of foreign investments in aiding its recovery efforts.

data.title: Mississippi College School of Law Introduces Mandatory AI Course for First-Year Students
data.description: Mississippi College School of Law has implemented a new requirement for all first-year students to complete a course on artificial intelligence. According to NS3.AI, this decision comes in response to growing concerns within the legal community about the reliability of AI-generated legal work. As both courts and law schools continue to adapt to the increasing use of AI, the course aims to equip future lawyers with the necessary skills to navigate and utilize AI technology effectively in their legal practices.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations are poised to impact liquidation levels significantly. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,420, the cumulative short liquidation intensity across major centralized exchanges could reach $1.166 billion. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $2,197, the cumulative long liquidation intensity could amount to $827 million.

data.title: Polymarket to Introduce Perpetual Contracts for Prediction Markets
data.description: Polymarket has announced plans to launch a perpetual contracts feature, allowing users to engage in prediction market trading with leverage. According to Odaily, the platform has opened early access registration for this new feature.

data.title: DoorDash Adopts Stablecoin Rails for Merchant Payouts
data.description: DoorDash is transitioning its merchant payouts to stablecoin rails on the Tempo network. According to NS3.AI, this move follows the processing of nearly $75 billion in merchant sales last year. Tempo has announced that Stripe, Coastal Bank, and ARQ are also shifting their payment flows to this network. The blockchain, developed by Stripe and Paradigm for handling payment workloads, became operational last month.

data.title: Polymarket has announced that it will be launching a perpetual contract feature.
data.description: Polymarket announced that it will launch a perpetual contract (Perps) feature, allowing users to leverage their participation in prediction market trading on the platform. Early access registration is now open.

data.title: EU Seeks to Ease Tensions with Turkey After Remarks by Commission President
data.description: The European Union is working to alleviate tensions with Turkey following comments made by European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen. Bloomberg posted on X that von der Leyen mentioned Turkey alongside Russia and China, which led to concerns that the NATO ally was being perceived as a potential threat. The EU is now focused on clarifying its stance and maintaining diplomatic relations with Turkey, emphasizing the importance of cooperation and partnership within the NATO framework. The situation highlights the delicate balance the EU must maintain in its foreign relations, particularly with key strategic partners.

data.title: Bitcoin Mentioned in Marvel's Daredevil: Born Again
data.description: Marvel's Daredevil: Born Again Season 2, Episode 5, features a reference to Bitcoin during a flashback scene set between 2014 and 2015. According to NS3.AI, the scene involves Wilson Fisk suggesting the use of Bitcoin to diversify money laundering operations. However, James Wesley dismisses the idea, citing Bitcoin's volatility, and recommends focusing on fine art instead.

data.title: Intel Shares Rise Following Analyst Upgrades Ahead of Earnings
data.description: Intel's stock experienced an uptick after receiving upgrades from BNP Paribas and HSBC. According to NS3.AI, BNP Paribas adjusted its rating to Neutral with a target price of $60, while HSBC upgraded Intel to Buy with a target of $95. These changes come ahead of Intel's anticipated earnings report on April 23. Wall Street analysts project Intel's first-quarter sales for 2026 to reach $12.38 billion, with expected earnings of $0.01 per share.
